title: Physics on the chart 📈
author: morriase
selftext:
Imagine a child swinging on a swing. If you track the path of their motion, it forms a loop in space. By looking at the shape of that loop, you can tell if the swing is gaining momentum (speeding up), slowing down, or swinging regularly.

The financial market behaves similarly: price movements, volatility, and trend strength trace out paths over time. The Quantum Geometric Phase Classifier takes these market paths and projects them onto a virtual three-dimensional sphere (called a Bloch Sphere, which represents a quantum bit or "qubit").

As the market moves, it draws a polygon path on this sphere. According to quantum mechanics, when a path completes a loop, it gains a special mathematical property called a geometric phase (specifically, a Berry Phase). This phase is directly proportional to the area enclosed by the path.

Our model measures the size of this enclosed area:

A large positive area indicates a high probability of an upward trend (Buy signal).

A large negative area indicates a high probability of a downward trend (Sell signal).

A narrow or zero area represents high uncertainty or a flat market (Hold/no-trade signal).

Passed the testing phase and is now in production.
